title: Asian Countries Limit Fuel Prices as Oil Gets Expensive
summary: Some countries in Asia are putting a limit on how much fuel can cost because oil prices are going up. This is happening because there is a war in the Middle East that makes it hard to get oil. Oil is a type of energy that cars and machines use. When oil is hard to get, it becomes more expensive. Countries like South Korea and Thailand are saying they will not let fuel prices go too high. They want to help people afford fuel for their cars and homes. Other countries like Vietnam and the Philippines are also doing things to save energy. For example, the Philippines has a shorter work week to use less energy. This is important because high fuel prices can make things cost more for everyone.

keywords.explanation: a type of energy used in cars and machines, like petrol or diesel
a natural resource that is used to make fuel and other products
a limit set by the government on how high a price can go
a large continent with many countries, including South Korea and Thailand
power that makes things work, like electricity or fuel

questions.question: Why are Asian countries limiting fuel prices?
options: Because oil prices are going up
Because they want to make more money
questions.correct_answer: Because oil prices are going up

questions.question: Which country said it will cap petrol prices?
What did Thailand plan to do with diesel prices?
How is the Philippines saving energy?
options: By having a four-day work week
questions.correct_answer: By having a four-day work week

questions.question: What can high fuel prices make more expensive?

background_read: Oil is a natural resource found underground that is used to make fuel like petrol and diesel. Fuel powers cars, buses, and machines. When there is a war or problem in places where oil comes from, like the Middle East, it can be hard to get oil, so prices go up. Governments sometimes step in to control prices to help people afford fuel for daily life. Asia is a continent with many countries that rely on oil from other places, so they are affected when prices rise.
Article_Structure: WHO: Governments in Asian countries like South Korea and Thailand
WHAT: They are capping fuel prices to limit how high they can go
WHERE: In Asia, including South Korea, Thailand, Vietnam, and the Philippines
WHY: Because oil prices are surging due to a war in the Middle East, making fuel expensive and hard to afford

perspectives.description: Governments want to protect people by controlling fuel prices during a crisis.
